title: Clippy Configuration
description:
Configuration file for [Clippy](https://doc.rust-lang.org/clippy/), the Rust linter. Placed at `clippy.toml` or `.clippy.toml` in the project root.

See the [lint configuration reference](https://doc.rust-lang.org/stable/clippy/lint_configuration.html) for full documentation.

Array options support the special `".."` element to extend the default list rather than replacing it.

x-lintel.catalogDescription: Configuration for Clippy, the Rust linter.

absolute-paths-allowed-crates.description: Which crates to allow absolute paths from. Use with the `absolute_paths` lint.

absolute-paths-max-segments.description: The maximum number of segments a path can have before being linted. Use with the `absolute_paths` lint.

accept-comment-above-attributes.description: Whether to accept a safety comment above the attributes for an `unsafe` block.

accept-comment-above-statement.description: Whether to accept a safety comment above the statement containing an `unsafe` block.

allow-comparison-to-zero.description: Don't lint when comparing the result of a modulo operation to zero.

allow-dbg-in-tests.description: Whether `dbg!` should be allowed in test functions or `#[cfg(test)]` code.

allow-exact-repetitions.description: Whether to allow items with the same name as the containing module.

allow-expect-in-consts.description: Whether `expect` should be allowed within `const` blocks evaluated at compile time.

allow-expect-in-tests.description: Whether `expect` should be allowed in test functions or `#[cfg(test)]` code.

allow-indexing-slicing-in-tests.description: Whether indexing and slicing should be allowed in test functions or `#[cfg(test)]` code.

allow-mixed-uninlined-format-args.description: Whether to allow mixed uninlined format args, e.g. `format!("{} {x}", x.y)`.

allow-one-hash-in-raw-strings.description: Whether to allow `r#""#` when `r""` can be used.

allow-panic-in-tests.description: Whether `panic` should be allowed in test functions or `#[cfg(test)]` code.

allow-print-in-tests.description: Whether print macros (e.g. `println!`) should be allowed in test functions or `#[cfg(test)]` code.

allow-private-module-inception.description: Whether to allow module inception if the module is not public.

allow-renamed-params-for.description: List of trait paths for which renamed parameter names are allowed. Supports the `".."` element to extend the default list.

allow-unwrap-in-consts.description: Whether `unwrap` should be allowed within `const` blocks evaluated at compile time.

allow-unwrap-in-tests.description: Whether `unwrap` should be allowed in test functions or `#[cfg(test)]` code.

allow-useless-vec-in-tests.description: Whether `useless_vec` should be ignored in test functions or `#[cfg(test)]` code.

allowed-dotfiles.description: Additional dotfiles (files or directories starting with a dot) to allow.

allowed-duplicate-crates.description: A list of crate names that are allowed to have multiple versions in the dependency tree.

allowed-idents-below-min-chars.description: Identifiers that are allowed to be shorter than `min-ident-chars-threshold`. Supports the `".."` element to extend the default list.

allowed-prefixes.description: List of prefixes to allow when checking for module name repetitions. Supports the `".."` element to extend the default list.

allowed-scripts.description: The list of Unicode scripts allowed in identifiers. Supports the `".."` element to extend the default list.

allowed-wildcard-imports.description: List of path segments allowed to have wildcard imports (e.g. `prelude`).

arithmetic-side-effects-allowed.description: Types to allow arithmetic operations on without linting. Supports the `".."` element to extend the default list.

arithmetic-side-effects-allowed-binary.description: Pairs of types `[left, right]` for which binary arithmetic operations are allowed. Use `"*"` as a wildcard to match any type.

arithmetic-side-effects-allowed-unary.description: Types for which unary arithmetic operations (negation, `!`) are allowed.

array-size-threshold.description: The maximum allowed size (in bytes) for arrays on the stack.

avoid-breaking-exported-api.description: Suppress lints whenever the suggested fix would cause breakage for other crates.

await-holding-invalid-types.description: Types that may not be held across `.await` points.

cargo-ignore-publish.description: For internal testing only. Whether to ignore the `publish` field in `Cargo.toml`.

check-incompatible-msrv-in-tests.description: Whether to also check MSRV compatibility in test code.

check-inconsistent-struct-field-initiali.description: Whether to suggest reordering constructor fields when some have shorthand initializers.

check-private-items.description: Whether to also run the applicable lint on private items.

cognitive-complexity-threshold.description: The maximum cognitive complexity a function can have.

const-literal-digits-threshold.description: Minimum number of digits in a float literal in a const context before linting.

disallowed-macros.description: List of macros that are not allowed to be used. Each entry can be a path string or an object with additional metadata.

disallowed-methods.description: List of methods that are not allowed to be used. Each entry can be a path string or an object with additional metadata.

disallowed-names.description: List of disallowed variable names (e.g. placeholder names). Supports the `".."` element to extend the default list.

disallowed-types.description: List of types that are not allowed to be used. Each entry can be a path string or an object with additional metadata.

doc-valid-idents.description: List of identifiers that are valid in documentation without backtick quoting. Supports the `".."` element to extend the default list.

enable-raw-pointer-heuristic-for-send.description: Whether to apply the raw pointer heuristic to determine if a type is `Send`.

enforce-iter-loop-reborrow.description: Whether to recommend using implicit `into_iter()` for reborrowed values in `for` loops.

enforced-import-renames.description: List of imports that must always be renamed. Each entry specifies a path and its required rename.

enum-variant-name-threshold.description: The minimum number of enum variants for the enum variant name lints to trigger.

enum-variant-size-threshold.description: The maximum size (in bytes) of an enum variant before suggesting boxing.

excessive-nesting-threshold.description: The maximum block nesting depth allowed before linting.

future-size-threshold.description: The maximum byte size of a `Future` before linting.

ignore-interior-mutability.description: Types to treat as if they have no interior mutability. Supports the `".."` element to extend the default list.

inherent-impl-lint-scope.description: The scope for checking duplicate inherent implementations.

large-error-ignored.description: Error types to ignore for the `result_large_err` lint.

large-error-threshold.description: The maximum size (in bytes) of the `Err` variant in a `Result` type.

lint-commented-code.description: Whether to lint collapsible `if`/`else` chains that contain comments between them.

literal-representation-threshold.description: The lower bound for linting decimal literals that are not human-readable.

matches-for-let-else.description: Which kind of `match` arms to consider when linting `manual_let_else`.

max-fn-params-bools.description: The maximum number of boolean parameters a function can have.

max-include-file-size.description: The maximum size (in bytes) of a file included via `include_bytes!` or `include_str!`.

max-struct-bools.description: The maximum number of boolean fields a struct can have.

max-suggested-slice-pattern-length.description: The maximum number of elements in a suggested slice pattern.

max-trait-bounds.description: The maximum number of trait bounds before suggesting a trait alias.

min-ident-chars-threshold.description: Minimum number of characters an identifier must have. Identifiers shorter than this are linted.

missing-docs-allow-unused.description: Whether to allow underscore-prefixed fields to skip documentation.

missing-docs-in-crate-items.description: Whether to only check items visible within the current crate for missing documentation.

module-item-order-groupings.description: Named groupings of source item kinds for the `arbitrary_source_item_ordering` lint. Each entry defines a group name and the item kinds it contains.

module-items-ordered-within-groupings.description: Whether items within groupings must be ordered alphabetically. Can be `"none"`, `"all"`, or a specific grouping name.

msrv.description: The minimum supported Rust version. Clippy will take this into account when suggesting alternatives that require a newer version. Use a version string like `"1.70.0"`.

pass-by-value-size-limit.description: Minimum size (in bytes) for a type to be passed by reference instead of by value.

pub-underscore-fields-behavior.description: Lint behavior for `pub` fields with underscore-prefixed names.

recursive-self-in-type-definitions.description: Whether to suggest replacing recursive type references with `Self`.

semicolon-inside-block-ignore-singleline.description: Whether to only lint multiline blocks for semicolons inside blocks.

semicolon-outside-block-ignore-multiline.description: Whether to only lint single-line blocks for semicolons outside blocks.

single-char-binding-names-threshold.description: The maximum number of single-character bindings a scope can have.

source-item-ordering.description: Which source item kinds to check for ordering within modules.

stack-size-threshold.description: The maximum stack size (in bytes) a function is allowed to use.

standard-macro-braces.description: Enforce specific brace styles for macro invocations. Each entry specifies a macro name and the brace character to enforce.

struct-field-name-threshold.description: The minimum number of struct fields for the struct field name lints to trigger.

suppress-restriction-lint-in-const.description: Whether to suppress restriction lints in constant code.

too-large-for-stack.description: The maximum object size (in bytes) on the stack before suggesting boxing.

too-many-arguments-threshold.description: The maximum number of parameters a function can have.

too-many-lines-threshold.description: The maximum number of lines a function body can have.

trait-assoc-item-kinds-order.description: The preferred ordering of associated items in a trait definition.

trivial-copy-size-limit.description: The maximum size (in bytes) of a `Copy` type that can be passed by value. Defaults to `target_pointer_width * 2` when unset.

type-complexity-threshold.description: The maximum complexity score a type can have before being linted.

unnecessary-box-size.description: The byte size below which `Box<T>` is unnecessary and the value should be unboxed.

unreadable-literal-lint-fractions.description: Whether to lint the decimal fraction part of float literals for readability.

upper-case-acronyms-aggressive.description: Whether to enable verbose/aggressive mode for upper-case acronym checking.

vec-box-size-threshold.description: The maximum size (in bytes) of a boxed type in a `Vec` before suggesting `Vec<T>` instead.

verbose-bit-mask-threshold.description: The maximum bit mask size before suggesting `.trailing_zeros()`.

warn-on-all-wildcard-imports.description: Whether to warn on all wildcard imports, including those from prelude modules.

warn-unsafe-macro-metavars-in-private-ma.description: Whether to warn about unsafe metavariable expansions in private `macro_rules!` macros.

DisallowedItem.description: A disallowed item (macro, method, or type) with optional reason and replacement.

path.description: Fully qualified path of the disallowed item (e.g. `std::vec::Vec::leak`).

reason.description: Explanation for why this item is disallowed. Shown in the lint message.

replacement.description: Suggested replacement to use instead. Shown in the lint message.

allow-invalid.description: If `true`, suppress the warning when the path cannot be resolved. Useful for conditionally compiled code.

DisallowedPath.description: A disallowed type path with optional reason.

reason.description: Explanation for why this type is disallowed.

ImportRename.description: An import that must be renamed when used.

path.description: Fully qualified path of the import (e.g. `std::process::exit`).

rename.description: The required rename for this import.

MacroBrace.description: Enforced brace style for a specific macro.

name.description: Macro name, either simple (e.g. `vec`) or fully qualified (e.g. `std::vec`).

brace.description: The brace character to enforce for this macro.

ModuleItemOrderGrouping.description: A named grouping of source item kinds for ordering within modules.

items.description: List of source item kinds in this group.
